channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: European Cloud Providers Back EU Push to Cut US Tech Reliance
item.description: Thirteen European cloud providers joined EU lawmakers and NGOs to support the European Commission initiative to reduce dependence on US technology, amid growing transatlantic tensions.

item.title: Russia Bans Aviation Fuel Exports as Ukrainian Strikes on Refineries Continue
item.description: The Russian government banned aviation fuel exports until November 30, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure continue to impact domestic supply.

item.title: Myanmar Village Explosion Kills at Least 55 as Rebel Army Site Detonates
item.description: At least 55 people were killed when explosives stored by a rebel army detonated in a Myanmar village, with witnesses describing widespread devastation as search efforts continue.

item.title: Netanyahu Orders Israeli Military to Strike Hezbollah Stronghold in Beirut
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the military to attack targets in Beirut southern suburbs, a Hezbollah stronghold known as Dahiyeh, as Israel deepens its Lebanon offensive.

item.title: China Coast Guard Patrols Waters East of Taiwan After Japan-Philippines Border Talks
item.description: China conducted law enforcement patrols east of Taiwan in response to Japan and the Philippines launching maritime border talks that overlap with areas claimed by China.

item.title: French Navy Intercepts Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker in Atlantic
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that the French Navy boarded a Russia-linked oil tanker subject to international sanctions, sailing from Russia.

item.title: Seven Injured in Iranian Missile Strike on US Base in Kuwait
item.description: Iran launched missile and drone attacks targeting US military bases in Kuwait, with Kuwaiti air defenses intercepting incoming threats amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

item.title: China Tightens Outbound Investment Rules After Meta-Manus Deal Fallout
item.description: China imposed new restrictions on outbound investments following controversy over AI company deals with Western tech firms, tightening capital controls.

item.title: UK Bars US Commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker Over Israel Remarks
item.description: Britain denied entry to two US political commentators over their statements regarding Israel, citing national security concerns.

item.title: German Retail Sales Fall Less Than Expected in April
item.description: German retail sales decreased by 0.3% in April compared to March, performing better than economists had forecast amid economic uncertainty from the Iran war.

item.title: US Gasoline Market Faces Test After Near-Record Stock Draws
item.description: Near-record gasoline inventory drawdowns are straining US fuel markets as seasonal demand peaks, raising concerns about supply buffers.

item.title: Philippine Senator Faces Second Arrest Order Over Graft Scandal
item.description: A Philippine senator charged with plunder stated he would surrender after a second arrest order was issued in connection with a major corruption case.

item.title: Markets Rise as US-Iran Exchange Fire But Keep Negotiating
item.description: Markets showed resilience as the US and Iran continued to trade military strikes while simultaneously negotiating an interim peace deal framework.

item.title: Right-Wing Outsider Leads Colombia Presidential Race
item.description: A pro-Trump outsider candidate advanced in Colombia's presidential election first round, raising questions about the country's political direction.

item.title: Five Killed in Explosion at Hanwha Aerospace Defense Plant in South Korea
item.description: An explosion at a Hanwha Aerospace defense factory in South Korea killed five workers, with authorities investigating the cause.

item.title: Trump Says Iran 'Really Wants' a Deal as Both Sides Exchange Fresh Strikes
item.description: President Trump stated that Iran genuinely wants to reach an agreement, even as US and Iranian forces continued to exchange military strikes across the region.

item.title: US Proposes New Israel-Lebanon De-escalation Plan Amid Fighting
item.description: The United States pushed a new ceasefire proposal for Lebanon and Israel as fighting intensified, with Israel deepening its offensive against Hezbollah.

item.title: Pro-Trump Outsider Wins First Round of Colombian Presidential Election
item.description: A right-wing outsider candidate aligned with Trump won the first round of Colombia's presidential election, disrupting the country's political establishment.

item.title: Nepal PM proposes dialogue to resolve India border dispute
item.description: Nepal's Prime Minister proposed diplomatic dialogue to resolve the longstanding Lipulekh border dispute with India amid rising tensions.

item.title: US strikes Iran sites, Tehran targets US base in escalating exchanges
item.description: The US military struck Iranian radar and drone sites while Tehran responded by targeting an American air base, marking another day of escalating hostilities.

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Military Sites, Tehran Retaliates Against American Base
item.description: The US conducted self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites, with Iran responding by attacking a US air base as the conflict entered day 94.

item.title: Israel Captures Strategic Beaufort Castle in Deeper Lebanon Incursion
item.description: Israeli troops captured the historic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon as part of their deeper push against Hezbollah, marking a significant strategic advance.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Oil Pipeline, Refinery and Fuel Depot
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck targets across multiple Russian regions including oil infrastructure, as Kyiv continues its campaign against Russian energy facilities.

item.title: Zelenskiy Seeks Peace Progress Before Winter as Ukraine Gains Strategic Ground
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskiy said he wants to advance peace talks with Russia before winter, citing Kyiv's improved strategic position on the battlefield.

item.title: Kuwait Intercepts Drone and Missile Attacks as Iran Conflict Spreads
item.description: Kuwait air defenses intercepted missiles and drones targeting US bases in the country, as the Iran-US conflict spread beyond the immediate combat zone.
